findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.render-template-string.render-template-string
findings.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.
cwe_ids: CWE-96: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')
findings.code_excerpt:
return render_template_string('''
Username:
Password:
''')

tool_specific.lines:
return render_template_string('''
Username:
Password:
''')

cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: python.jwt.security.jwt-hardcode.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: javascript.jsonwebtoken.security.jwt-hardcode.hardcoded-jwt-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: python.jwt.security.jwt-hardcode.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: python.flask.security.open-redirect.open-redirect
findings.description: Data from request is passed to redirect(). This is an open redirect and could be exploited. Consider using 'url_for()' to generate links to known locations. If you must use a URL to unknown pages, consider using 'urlparse()' or similar and checking if the 'netloc' property is the same as your site's host name. See the references for more information.
cwe_ids: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled

findings.title: python.boto3.security.hardcoded-token.hardcoded-token
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: python.lang.security.audit.formatted-sql-query.formatted-sql-query
findings.description: Detected possible formatted SQL query. Use parameterized queries instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.title: python.lang.security.audit.sqli.psycopg-sqli.psycopg-sqli
findings.description: Detected string concatenation with a non-literal variable in a psycopg2 Python SQL statement. This could lead to SQL injection if the variable is user-controlled and not properly sanitized. In order to prevent SQL injection, use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead. You can use prepared statements by creating a 'sql.SQL' string. You can also use the pyformat binding style to create parameterized queries. For example: 'cur.execute(SELECT * FROM table WHERE name=%s, user_input)'
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.title: python.sqlalchemy.security.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query
findings.description: Avoiding SQL string concatenation: untrusted input concatenated with raw SQL query can result in SQL Injection. In order to execute raw query safely, prepared statement should be used. SQLAlchemy provides TextualSQL to easily used prepared statement with named parameters. For complex SQL composition, use SQL Expression Language or Schema Definition Language. In most cases, SQLAlchemy ORM will be a better option.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: python.django.security.injection.ssrf.ssrf-injection-requests.ssrf-injection-requests
findings.description: Data from request object is passed to a new server-side request. This could lead to a server-side request forgery (SSRF). To mitigate, ensure that schemes and hosts are validated against an allowlist, do not forward the response to the user, and ensure proper authentication and transport-layer security in the proxied request. See https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Server_Side_Request_Forgery to learn more about SSRF vulnerabilities.

findings.title: python.flask.security.injection.ssrf-requests.ssrf-requests
findings.description: Data from request object is passed to a new server-side request. This could lead to a server-side request forgery (SSRF). To mitigate, ensure that schemes and hosts are validated against an allowlist, do not forward the response to the user, and ensure proper authentication and transport-layer security in the proxied request.

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.directly-returned-format-string.directly-returned-format-string
findings.description: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: python.django.security.injection.raw-html-format.raw-html-format
findings.description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`django.shortcuts.render`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: python.flask.security.injection.raw-html-concat.raw-html-format
findings.description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`flask.render_template`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse.use-defused-xml-parse
findings.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
